criteria.name: AI Code Generation Tools Focus
criteria.description: The study must specifically investigate Large Language Model (LLM)-based tools or AI assistants designed for code generation, completion, or refactoring (e.g., GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, Amazon CodeWhisperer) as the primary intervention.

criteria.name: Developer Productivity or Performance Outcomes
criteria.description: The paper must provide empirical data or measurable metrics regarding the tool's impact on software developer productivity, such as task completion time, effort reduction, or development speed.

criteria.name: Code Quality and Artifact Evaluation
criteria.description: The research must evaluate the output of AI tools in terms of correctness, maintainability, readability, or structural integrity through automated testing or manual expert review.

criteria.name: Methodological and Metric Identification
criteria.description: The study must explicitly detail the evaluation methodology (e.g., controlled experiment, case study) and the metrics used to assess AI-assisted programming to contribute to the classification of research standards.

criteria.name: Risks, Vulnerabilities, and Limitations
criteria.description: The study must analyze specific challenges, security vulnerabilities (e.g., CWEs), hallucinations, or negative behavioral impacts (e.g., over-reliance) associated with the use of AI tools in development.

criteria.name: Lack of Empirical Validation
criteria.description: Exclude papers that are purely conceptual, theoretical, or anecdotal, such as opinion pieces, editorials, or essays that do not present systematic data collection or evidence-based results.

criteria.name: Non-Programming NLP Tasks
criteria.description: Exclude studies focused on general natural language processing (NLP) tasks (e.g., translation, general sentiment analysis) that are not applied to software engineering, source code, or programming tasks.

criteria.name: Inaccessible Research Context
criteria.description: Exclude papers that lack sufficient detail regarding the study design, tool configuration, or measurement framework, preventing a reliable assessment of the impact on developers or code.
